description_long: This is a rugged, military-style electronic device housed in a rectangular, olive-green metal case. The front panel features a large central rotary dial, several smaller knobs, toggle switches, and analog meters, as well as a black instruction plate with Cyrillic text. The top and sides are plain, with visible latches, hinges, and reinforced corners, suggesting the case is designed for durability and transport. The device appears to be a specialized piece of equipment, possibly a field radio, signal analyzer, or measurement instrument, with a utilitarian design and robust construction. The front panel is the only side with controls and indicators, while the other sides are featureless except for hardware for carrying or securing the unit. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a matte, slightly textured finish and minimal decorative elements.
description: A rugged, olive-green electronic device with a control panel featuring dials, switches, and meters, housed in a durable metal case. The design is utilitarian and built for field or industrial use.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main control panel with a large rotary dial, analog meters, switches, and a black instruction plate with Cyrillic text.
description_view_1: The left side view is plain, showing only the metal casing and some latches and hinges on the top edge.
description_view_2: The rear view is featureless, displaying a solid olive-green metal surface with no controls or openings, only some hardware on the top.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, with a plain metal surface and visible latches and hinges on the top edge.
attribute_control_interface: rotary dial, physical buttons, toggle switches
